title: Dog Ate Avocado Pit: Risks, Emergency Steps, and Veterinary Advice
meta_desc: If your dog swallowed an avocado pit, find out why this is a medium-urgency situation and how to monitor for signs of a gastrointestinal blockage.
content_html:
TL;DR: An ingested avocado pit is a medium-urgency emergency due to the high risk of choking or gastrointestinal blockage. Contact your veterinarian immediately, especially if your dog is gagging or having difficulty breathing.

What are the health risks if my dog swallows an avocado pit?

  • Avocado pits pose a severe choking hazard and a significant risk for gastrointestinal obstruction.
  • The pit is extremely hard and cannot be broken down by your dog's stomach acid.
  • While pits contain the toxin persin, the physical blockage is the most immediate and dangerous concern for canines.

Is it an emergency if my dog ate an avocado pit?

  • The urgency level for this situation is Medium; you should contact a veterinarian or emergency clinic as soon as possible.
  • The pit can become lodged in the narrow passages of the digestive tract even if your dog is currently acting normally.
  • If your dog is coughing, gagging, or having trouble breathing, the situation is a high-priority emergency.

How can providing a photo help the veterinary triage team?

  • A photo of the avocado variety or a similar-sized pit helps the team compare the object size to your dog's breed and weight.
  • This visual aid helps the vet estimate the risk of the pit passing through the pylorus.
  • It assists the veterinarian in deciding if inducing vomiting is safe or if immediate diagnostic imaging is required.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Although dogs appear relatively resistant to avocado toxicosis compared to other species, ingestion of the fruit, leaves, stems, and seeds of avocado has been associated with toxicosis in animals. The toxic principle in avocado, persin, can cause myocardial necrosis at high dosages. Diagnosis of avocado toxicosis relies on a history of exposure and clinical signs, as there are no readily available specific tests. Differential diagnoses include other causes of mastitis and other myocardial insults.

Chapter: Toxicology, Cardiology

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 2965)

faqs.question: Can I induce vomiting at home?
faqs.answer: You should never induce vomiting without professional guidance. Because of its size and shape, an avocado pit can easily become lodged in the esophagus on the way back up, which is a life-threatening situation.

faqs.question: What symptoms of a blockage should I watch for?
faqs.answer: Keep a close eye out for repeated vomiting, lethargy, loss of appetite, abdominal pain, or an inability to keep water down, as these are signs of a total obstruction.

faqs.question: Will the pit dissolve in my dog's stomach?
faqs.answer: No, avocado pits are extremely durable and will not be broken down by stomach acid. They must either be vomited up (under vet supervision), passed in the stool, or surgically removed.
